name: Terry v. Ohio
summary: Police may conduct limited search for weapons (stop and frisk) based on reasonable suspicion that person is armed and dangerous; established reasonable suspicion standard distinct from probable cause.
holding: Brief investigatory stop and protective frisk permissible when officer has reasonable suspicion based on specific articulable facts that criminal activity may be afoot and person may be armed and dangerous.
relevance_to_audit: Foundational case establishing reasonable suspicion standard; relevant to analyzing investigatory actions, temporary detentions, and limited searches in administrative context.
